title: Read N Integers and Print Them in Reverse
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read N integers from stdin into a `std::vector<int>`, then print them in reverse order, one per line.

## Input
- Line 1: integer `N` (1 ≤ N ≤ 20).
- Lines 2 through N+1: one integer per line.

## Output
- Print exactly N lines: the integers in reverse order (last-entered first).

## Requirements
1. Build the vector using `push_back` inside a loop — do not construct with a fixed size.
2. After reading all N integers, iterate from the last element to the first and print each on its own line.
3. Hidden tests use N=1 (single element) and N=5 with negative values.

title: Capped Average — Complete the Function
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `cappedAverage` that takes a `std::vector<int>` by const reference and an integer cap, resizes the internal working copy so it contains only the first `cap` elements (or all elements if the vector is smaller than `cap`), then returns the average of those elements as a `double`.

## Input
- Line 1: integer `N` (1 ≤ N ≤ 20).
- Line 2: N space-separated integers.
- Line 3: integer `cap` (1 ≤ cap ≤ 20).

## Output
- Print exactly one line: the average rounded to 2 decimal places, e.g. `Average: 3.50`.

## Requirements
1. The function signature must be `double cappedAverage(const std::vector<int>& data, int cap)`.
2. Inside the function, make a local copy of `data`, then call `resize` on the copy so it has `min(cap, (int)data.size())` elements.
3. Sum the elements of the resized copy and divide by its new size to get the average.
4. `main` reads N, reads N integers, reads cap, calls `cappedAverage`, and prints the result.
5. Hidden tests: cap larger than N (use all elements); cap=1.

test_cases.label: Hidden: cap larger than N (use all)
